name: Aranda Comarca Loop
name_original: Calcena - Illueca - Morata de Jalón - Mesones de Isuela - Trasobares — circular por la Comarca de Aranda
description: This difficult 50.5-mile road cycling route through the Aranda Comarca offers challenging climbs and diverse landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Calcena traverse the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park, within the Iberian System. The region features a diverse landscape of massifs, plateaus, and valleys. Roads are often sinuous and wind through hilly terrain, offering varied climbs and descents. This area provides a dynamic environment for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Calcena The most popular road cycling route is Aranda Comarca Loop, a 50.5 miles (81.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 52 minutes to complete. This route features varied terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Templars' Path Loop, a moderate 24.6 miles (39.6 km) path. It offers a mix of climbs and descents through the region's hilly landscape. Local road cyclists also love the Beratón Pass via Cara Oculta del Moncayo, a 31.6 miles (50.8 km) trail leading through the Moncayo Natural Park foothills, often completed in about 2 hours 37 minutes. Road cycling around Calcena is defined by sinuous roads, hilly terrain, and views of the Iberian System. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 90 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Calcena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Calcena?
A: Road cycling routes around Calcena are characterized by a diverse and rugged landscape, including massifs, plateaus, and valleys. You'll encounter sinuous single-track roads that wind through picturesque scenery, offering a dynamic experience with both challenging climbs and descents through hilly terrain.

Q: Are there routes suitable for different skill levels in Calcena?
A: Yes, the routes around Calcena cater to moderate and difficult ability levels. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992909482/templars-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Templars' Path Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate option, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989952691/aranda-comarca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranda Comarca Loop&lt;/a&gt; presents a more difficult challenge with significant elevation gain.

Q: What are some of the most scenic road cycling routes in the Calcena area?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Iberian System. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e988553529/beraton-pass-via-cara-oculta-del-moncayo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beratón Pass via Cara Oculta del Moncayo&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park, providing panoramic vistas. The region's 'magical valley' with its pinnacles and crags also forms a visually striking backdrop for many rides.

Q: Can I find long-distance road cycling routes in Calcena?
A: Yes, Calcena offers options for longer rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar de Moncayo – Castillo de Vozmediano loop from Tierga&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route spanning over 150 km, ideal for those seeking an extensive cycling adventure.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks or viewpoints to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The routes often pass by significant natural features. You can find highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5291398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de la Mata&lt;/a&gt; viewpoint, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valcongosto Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, and various summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5288052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Morrón Summit&lt;/a&gt;, offering breathtaking views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Calcena?
A: Given the hilly and rugged terrain, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Calcena, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder weather to the higher elevations of the Iberian System.

Q: Are there any loop routes available for road cyclists?
A: Yes, many routes around Calcena are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e989952691/aranda-comarca-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aranda Comarca Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077398339/borobia-ciria-and-malanquilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route in Calcena?
A: While specific parking areas for each route are not detailed, Calcena is a small village. You can typically find parking within the village itself or at designated spots near the start of popular routes. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What makes road cycling in Calcena unique compared to other regions?
A: Calcena's uniqueness lies in its location within the southern foothills of the Moncayo Natural Park and the Iberian System. This provides a combination of quiet, sinuous roads, challenging inclines, and panoramic views of a rugged, diverse landscape, including dramatic geological formations like pinnacles and crags.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Calcena area?
A: There are 10 road cycling routes available around Calcena, offering a variety of distances and difficulty levels for exploration.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through villages or offer opportunities for refreshments?
A: Many routes in the region connect various villages. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1077398339/borobia-ciria-and-malanquilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borobia, Ciria and Malanquilla Loop&lt;/a&gt; passes through multiple settlements, providing potential opportunities to stop for refreshments or a meal in local cafes or pubs.
tips: From this point, on the outskirts of Calcena, you have a good view of the whole town with its traditional houses and its magnificent natural environment.
At this point we have a magnificent picture following the GR 90.2 through the Calcena ravine. Nearby we will find the deep cave or the town of Calcena itself

A charming place with a lot of history is the one that we find perched on top of the rock in the town of Illueca. It is the Castle - Palace of Pope Luna (who was born here) that stands out from afar. They make very interesting guided tours (prior reservation) based on the history of the palace itself and of Papa Luna.
There is restaurant and hotel service

We are in Plaza España in the town of Morata de Jalón, a wonderful historical place that is undoubtedly worth a walk. We will see the imposing church of Santa Ana and the Renaissance-Baroque palace (now town hall).

From this point we have a beautiful picture of the imposing Castle of Mesones de Isuela controlling the town from the top of the rocky hill.
Guided tours inside the castle can be booked at the tourist office of the Aranda region

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Calcena - Illueca - Morata de Jalón - Mesones de Isuela - Trasobares circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging road cycling route typically takes around 3 hours and 53 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as a "difficult" road ride and requires a very good level of fitness due to its significant distance and elevation gain. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is generally characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces, making it easy to ride for road cyclists. While the broader region has mountain biking trails with varied terrain, this specific road cycling route focuses on paved roads.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Calcena - Illueca - Morata de Jalón - Mesones de Isuela - Trasobares route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter diverse landscapes and notable landmarks. Highlights include a View of Calcena, a View of Barranco de Calcena from GR 90.2, the historic Castle-Palace of Pope Luna, and the Plaza de España and Church of Santa Ana in Morata de Jalón. You can also enjoy a View of Castillo de Mesones de Isuela. The scenery includes riverside vegetation, historic abandoned vineyards, and lush kermes oak forests, with expansive panoramic views from higher points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Aranda Comarca region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the hottest summer months. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check local weather patterns before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route passing through several towns, you can typically find parking in any of the main villages along the way, such as Calcena, Illueca, Morata de Jalón, Mesones de Isuela, or Trasobares. Parking in town centers is usually available.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Parque Natural del Moncayo?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural del Moncayo. However, always respect local regulations and protected area guidelines.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While road cycling itself isn't typically an activity for dogs, if you plan to stop in the towns, be aware of local regulations regarding dogs. It's best to leave your furry friends at home for this challenging cycling excursion.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through several towns including Calcena, Illueca, Morata de Jalón, Mesones de Isuela, and Trasobares. These villages may offer local cafes, restaurants, or small accommodations where you can refuel or stay overnight.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this road cycling route overlap with or are in close proximity to other official trails, including Etapa 4: Calcena - Tierga, Etapa 5: Tierga - Morata de Jalón, and Etapa 1: Calcena - Aranda de Moncayo.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this ride?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque countryside largely untouched by mass tourism. You'll experience varied natural beauty, including riverside vegetation, historic abandoned vineyards, and lush kermes oak forests. Keep an eye out for expansive panoramic views, especially from higher vantage points like the vicinity of the chapel of San Cristóbal de Calcena.
